rationale: Both correctly diagnose the config/tokenizer mismatch, cite the same crash line, confirm the DiT workaround, and defer the guard/docs fix to a follow-up issue per Gaohan123 — matching the thread's resolution. Y earns a grounding edge by explicitly citing PR #2713 (the exact reference akshatvishu gave for why the topology changed), which X omits, but Y also asserts an unverified specific reason (VAE resolution bins for IT2I/T2I) not established in the thread. X is the more complete and usable answer: it gives a concrete code-level guard implementation, flags the misleading YAML headers, and reads as a clean, postable maintainer comment, whereas Y's output is cluttered with duplicated draft_answer blocks and raw agent-report metadata (task dict, confidence, blockers, assumptions) that would need heavy editing before posting.
